tags.qa_notes: Depth corrected from 26m to 18m per multiple independent sources (PADI, Cortez Club, La Paz Divers, DiversInnMX). 26m was not confirmed by any source. Population 600 confirmed by TOSEA research.

Three pinnacle tops at 16m, 25m, 21m (confirmed Wikipedia Marisla Seamount article). Hammerhead species confirmed as scalloped hammerhead by multiple sources. 40m max working depth confirmed as Advanced.
Depth 12m (35-40ft) consistent across sources. Marine life re-audited: coral hawkfish and damselfish confirmed at site by DAN and UWPhotoGuide. Garden eels in surrounding sand confirmed.

Wreck history re-audited. Vessel dimensions 320ft x 50ft confirmed by UCR primary source. 12-foot gash claim removed (not in primary source). Hurricane Liza Sept 30 1976 confirmed. All core facts hold. LST origin and ferry route confirmed.
Site type wall confirmed. 35m max depth consistent. Marine life re-audited: stonefish claim removed (not site-specific). Sea fan and rock fish coverage confirmed by multiple operators.

Depth 25m (80ft) confirmed by Baja Diving and Cortez Club. PADI lists 27m max. Manta season corrected to mid-June through October per Cabo Private Guide and La Paz Divers. Unverified '15-year hiatus' claim removed. Sea lion colony ~40 confirmed.
Depth 15-16m confirmed. Coral restoration figures (7,500 colonies + 5,000 nursery) confirmed by Yatezzitos and Power Traveller. Sea lion colony 40-50 non-breeding animals confirmed.

Wreck history re-audited against 4 independent sources. Single vessel confirmed (Cortez Club reference to two vessels conflates Fang Ming with C-59, a separate 2004 wreck). Capture April 1995 and sinking Nov 18 1999 confirmed by 3+ sources. Length ~56m (180ft) consistent. 'First intentional artificial reef in Latin America' confirmed by multiple sources. Green sea turtles, angelfish, groupers confirmed at site.

Site primarily liveaboard. Hammerheads confirmed by ScubaBoard trip reports (Oct 2023 at 124ft/38m). Sea lion colony on island confirmed by Nautilus liveaboard report. Mobula ray season claim for May-July not directly confirmed at this specific site - phrasing softened to 'regional'. 28m working depth retained as conservative figure; deeper dives to 38m+ are possible.

Identified as 'La Punta Norte' on Baja Diving site. Depth 18m (60ft) confirmed by Baja Diving and DiversInnMX. Marine life re-audited: sea turtles, barracuda, moray eels, sergeant majors confirmed. Pelagic visitors (hammerheads, tuna, wahoo) noted as occasional per Baja Diving and Cabo Private Guide.
